questions.question: The particles in a substance are far apart, randomly arranged and moving. The substance changes state and the particles are now close together. The particles are still randomly arranged and able to move. What is the change of state of the substance? A gas to liquid, B liquid to gas, C liquid to solid, D solid to gas
questions.text: The particles in a substance are far apart, randomly arranged and moving. The substance changes state and the particles are now close together. The particles are still randomly arranged and able to move. What is the change of state of the substance? A gas to liquid, B liquid to gas, C liquid to solid, D solid to gas

questions.question: A student put exactly 25.00 cm 3 of dilute hydrochloric acid into a conical flask. The student added 2.5 g of solid sodium carbonate and measured the change in temperature of the mixture. Which apparatus does the student need to use? A balance, measuring cylinder, thermometer, B balance, pipette, stopwatch, C balance, pipette, thermometer, D burette, pipette, thermometer
questions.text: A student put exactly 25.00 cm 3 of dilute hydrochloric acid into a conical flask. The student added 2.5 g of solid sodium carbonate and measured the change in temperature of the mixture. Which apparatus does the student need to use? A balance, measuring cylinder, thermometer, B balance, pipette, stopwatch, C balance, pipette, thermometer, D burette, pipette, thermometer

questions.question: A student separates sugar from pieces of broken glass by dissolving the sugar in water and filtering off the broken glass. filtrate What is the filtrate? A broken glass only, B broken glass and sugar solution, C pure water, D sugar solution 3
questions.text: A student separates sugar from pieces of broken glass by dissolving the sugar in water and filtering off the broken glass. filtrate What is the filtrate? A broken glass only, B broken glass and sugar solution, C pure water, D sugar solution 3
options: B broken glass and sugar solution

questions.question: The nucleus of a particular atom consists of nineteen particles. Nine of them are positively charged and ten of them are uncharged. Which statement about this nucleus is correct? A The nucleus has a nucleon number of nine., B The nucleus has a nucleon number of ten., C The nucleus has a proton number of nine., D The nucleus has a proton number of ten.
questions.text: The nucleus of a particular atom consists of nineteen particles. Nine of them are positively charged and ten of them are uncharged. Which statement about this nucleus is correct? A The nucleus has a nucleon number of nine., B The nucleus has a nucleon number of ten., C The nucleus has a proton number of nine., D The nucleus has a proton number of ten.
options: A The nucleus has a nucleon number of nine.
B The nucleus has a nucleon number of ten.
C The nucleus has a proton number of nine.
D The nucleus has a proton number of ten.

questions.question: Which description of brass is correct? A alloy, B compound, C element, D non-metal
questions.text: Which description of brass is correct? A alloy, B compound, C element, D non-metal

questions.question: A Group I element combines with a Group VII element and forms an ionic bond. Which row shows how the electronic structures change? Group I element Group VII element before bonding after bonding before bonding after bonding A 2,8,1 2,8,2 2,7 2,6, B 2,8 2,7 2,8 2,8,1, C 2,8,1 2,8 2,7 2,8, D 2,8 2,8,1 2,8 2,7 4
questions.text: A Group I element combines with a Group VII element and forms an ionic bond. Which row shows how the electronic structures change? Group I element Group VII element before bonding after bonding before bonding after bonding A 2,8,1 2,8,2 2,7 2,6, B 2,8 2,7 2,8 2,8,1, C 2,8,1 2,8 2,7 2,8, D 2,8 2,8,1 2,8 2,7 4

questions.question: Four covalent compounds are listed. chlorine methane ammonia water Which row identifies the total number of covalent bonds in each compound? chlorine    methane    ammonia    water A 2 4 3 2, B 1 3 2 2, C 2 3 2 3, D 1 4 3 2
questions.text: Four covalent compounds are listed. chlorine methane ammonia water Which row identifies the total number of covalent bonds in each compound? chlorine    methane    ammonia    water A 2 4 3 2, B 1 3 2 2, C 2 3 2 3, D 1 4 3 2

questions.question: Magnesium reacts with dilute hydrochloric acid to produce a salt and hydrogen gas. What is the equation for this reaction? A Mg  +  HCl    MgCl  +  H, B Mg  +  H 2 Cl 2   MgCl 2 +  H 2, C Mg  +  2HCl    MgCl 2 +  H 2, D Mg  +  2HCl    MgCl 2 +  2H
questions.text: Magnesium reacts with dilute hydrochloric acid to produce a salt and hydrogen gas. What is the equation for this reaction? A Mg  +  HCl    MgCl  +  H, B Mg  +  H 2 Cl 2   MgCl 2 +  H 2, C Mg  +  2HCl    MgCl 2 +  H 2, D Mg  +  2HCl    MgCl 2 +  2H
options: B Mg  +  H 2 Cl 2   MgCl 2 +  H 2

questions.question: The formula of sodium chlorate( V) is NaCl O 3 . What is the relative formula mass of sodium chlorate( V), NaCl O 3 ? A 52.0 B  74.5 C  106.5 D  223.5
questions.text: The formula of sodium chlorate( V) is NaCl O 3 . What is the relative formula mass of sodium chlorate( V), NaCl O 3 ? A 52.0 B  74.5 C  106.5 D  223.5

questions.question: Effervescence  is  observed  at  the  negative  electrode  (cathode)  during  the  electrolysis  of concentrated aqueous sodium chloride. Which element is produced at the negative electrode (cathode)? A chlorine, B hydrogen, C oxygen, D sodium 5
questions.text: Effervescence  is  observed  at  the  negative  electrode  (cathode)  during  the  electrolysis  of concentrated aqueous sodium chloride. Which element is produced at the negative electrode (cathode)? A chlorine, B hydrogen, C oxygen, D sodium 5

questions.question: The energy level diagram for a chemical reaction is shown. energy progress of reaction reactants products Which statement about this reaction is correct? A The reaction is endothermic and energy is given out to the surroundings., B The reaction is endothermic and energy is taken in from the surroundings., C The reaction is exothermic and energy is given out to the surroundings., D The reaction is exothermic and energy is taken in from the surroundings.
questions.text: The energy level diagram for a chemical reaction is shown. energy progress of reaction reactants products Which statement about this reaction is correct? A The reaction is endothermic and energy is given out to the surroundings., B The reaction is endothermic and energy is taken in from the surroundings., C The reaction is exothermic and energy is given out to the surroundings., D The reaction is exothermic and energy is taken in from the surroundings.
options: A The reaction is endothermic and energy is given out to the surroundings.
B The reaction is endothermic and energy is taken in from the surroundings.
C The reaction is exothermic and energy is given out to the surroundings.
D The reaction is exothermic and energy is taken in from the surroundings.

questions.question: Which property explains why methane is used as a fuel? A It is an alkane., B It forms carbon dioxide when it burns., C It is a gas at room temperature., D It releases heat energy when it burns.
questions.text: Which property explains why methane is used as a fuel? A It is an alkane., B It forms carbon dioxide when it burns., C It is a gas at room temperature., D It releases heat energy when it burns.
options: B It forms carbon dioxide when it burns.
C It is a gas at room temperature.
D It releases heat energy when it burns.

questions.question: Solid copper( II) carbonate reacts with dilute sulfuric acid. CuCO 3 +  H 2 SO 4   CuSO 4 +  CO 2 +  H 2 O The rate of the reaction can be changed by varying the conditions. Which changes always increase the rate of this chemical reaction? 1   increasing the concentration of sulfuric acid 2   increasing the size of the pieces of copper( II) carbonate 3   increasing the temperature 4   increasing the volume of sulfuric acid A 1, 3 and 4 B  1 and 3 only C  2 and 3 D  3 and 4 only 6
questions.text: Solid copper( II) carbonate reacts with dilute sulfuric acid. CuCO 3 +  H 2 SO 4   CuSO 4 +  CO 2 +  H 2 O The rate of the reaction can be changed by varying the conditions. Which changes always increase the rate of this chemical reaction? 1   increasing the concentration of sulfuric acid 2   increasing the size of the pieces of copper( II) carbonate 3   increasing the temperature 4   increasing the volume of sulfuric acid A 1, 3 and 4 B  1 and 3 only C  2 and 3 D  3 and 4 only 6
options: A 1, 3 and 4 B  1 and 3 only C  2 and 3 D  3 and 4 only 6

questions.question: Magnesium carbonate and hydrochloric acid react to form a gas. The volume of gas is measured at fixed time intervals. In  experiment  1,  an  excess  of  magnesium  carbonate  granules  reacts  with  100 cm 3 of hydrochloric acid. The experiment is repeated but with one change. This is experiment 2. The results are shown on the graph. volume of gas 0 0 time experiment 2 experiment 1 Which change is made? A A catalyst is added., B Magnesium carbonate powder is used., C The hydrochloric acid is more concentrated., D The temperature is lower.
questions.text: Magnesium carbonate and hydrochloric acid react to form a gas. The volume of gas is measured at fixed time intervals. In  experiment  1,  an  excess  of  magnesium  carbonate  granules  reacts  with  100 cm 3 of hydrochloric acid. The experiment is repeated but with one change. This is experiment 2. The results are shown on the graph. volume of gas 0 0 time experiment 2 experiment 1 Which change is made? A A catalyst is added., B Magnesium carbonate powder is used., C The hydrochloric acid is more concentrated., D The temperature is lower.
options: B Magnesium carbonate powder is used.
C The hydrochloric acid is more concentrated.

questions.question: Which colour change is observed when water is added to anhydrous cobalt( II) chloride? A blue to pink, B pink to blue, C blue to white, D white to blue
questions.text: Which colour change is observed when water is added to anhydrous cobalt( II) chloride? A blue to pink, B pink to blue, C blue to white, D white to blue

questions.question: The equation for the extraction of iron from its ore is shown. Fe 2 O 3 +  3CO    2Fe  +  3CO 2 Which statement is correct? A Iron is oxidised., B Iron( III) oxide is oxidised., C Carbon monoxide is oxidised., D Carbon monoxide is reduced. 7
questions.text: The equation for the extraction of iron from its ore is shown. Fe 2 O 3 +  3CO    2Fe  +  3CO 2 Which statement is correct? A Iron is oxidised., B Iron( III) oxide is oxidised., C Carbon monoxide is oxidised., D Carbon monoxide is reduced. 7
options: B Iron( III) oxide is oxidised.

questions.question: Solution X is tested separately with sodium carbonate and litmus. Which row shows that X is acidic? sodium carbonate litmus A effervescence blue, B effervescence red, C no change blue, D no change red
questions.text: Solution X is tested separately with sodium carbonate and litmus. Which row shows that X is acidic? sodium carbonate litmus A effervescence blue, B effervescence red, C no change blue, D no change red

questions.question: Basic oxides are neutralised by acidic oxides. Which element forms an oxide that neutralises calcium oxide? A hydrogen, B magnesium, C sodium, D sulfur
questions.text: Basic oxides are neutralised by acidic oxides. Which element forms an oxide that neutralises calcium oxide? A hydrogen, B magnesium, C sodium, D sulfur

questions.question: Which method produces a pure sample of copper( II) sulfate crystals? A Add an excess of copper( II) carbonate to dilute sulfuric acid, filter and evaporate the filtrate until crystals start to appear., B Add an excess of copper( II) carbonate to dilute sulfuric acid, filter off the remaining solid and dry it in an oven at 100 C., C Warm an excess of copper( II) oxide with dilute sulfuric acid and evaporate the mixture to dryness., D Warm an excess of copper( II) oxide with dilute sulfuric acid and filter off the crystals formed.
questions.text: Which method produces a pure sample of copper( II) sulfate crystals? A Add an excess of copper( II) carbonate to dilute sulfuric acid, filter and evaporate the filtrate until crystals start to appear., B Add an excess of copper( II) carbonate to dilute sulfuric acid, filter off the remaining solid and dry it in an oven at 100 C., C Warm an excess of copper( II) oxide with dilute sulfuric acid and evaporate the mixture to dryness., D Warm an excess of copper( II) oxide with dilute sulfuric acid and filter off the crystals formed.
options: A Add an excess of copper( II) carbonate to dilute sulfuric acid, filter and evaporate the filtrate until crystals start to appear.
B Add an excess of copper( II) carbonate to dilute sulfuric acid, filter off the remaining solid and dry it in an oven at 100 C.
C Warm an excess of copper( II) oxide with dilute sulfuric acid and evaporate the mixture to dryness.
D Warm an excess of copper( II) oxide with dilute sulfuric acid and filter off the crystals formed.

questions.question: Which statement about aqueous sodium hydroxide is correct? A When it is added to a solution containing sulfate ions, a white precipitate is formed., B When it is added to a solution of copper( II) ions, a blue precipitate is formed which dissolves in excess to give deep blue solution., C When it is added to a solution of iron( II) ions, a green precipitate is formed which does not dissolve in excess., D When it is added to ammonium chloride, a gas is produced which turns blue litmus red. 8
questions.text: Which statement about aqueous sodium hydroxide is correct? A When it is added to a solution containing sulfate ions, a white precipitate is formed., B When it is added to a solution of copper( II) ions, a blue precipitate is formed which dissolves in excess to give deep blue solution., C When it is added to a solution of iron( II) ions, a green precipitate is formed which does not dissolve in excess., D When it is added to ammonium chloride, a gas is produced which turns blue litmus red. 8
options: A When it is added to a solution containing sulfate ions, a white precipitate is formed.
B When it is added to a solution of copper( II) ions, a blue precipitate is formed which dissolves in excess to give deep blue solution.
C When it is added to a solution of iron( II) ions, a green precipitate is formed which does not dissolve in excess.
D When it is added to ammonium chloride, a gas is produced which turns blue litmus red. 8

questions.question: A period of the Periodic Table is shown. group    I   II   III   IV   V   VI   VII  VIII element   R   S   T   V   W   X   Y   Z The letters are not their chemical symbols. Which statement is correct? A Element R does not conduct electricity., B Elements R and Y react together to form an ionic compound., C Element Z exists as a diatomic molecule., D Element Z reacts with element T.
questions.text: A period of the Periodic Table is shown. group    I   II   III   IV   V   VI   VII  VIII element   R   S   T   V   W   X   Y   Z The letters are not their chemical symbols. Which statement is correct? A Element R does not conduct electricity., B Elements R and Y react together to form an ionic compound., C Element Z exists as a diatomic molecule., D Element Z reacts with element T.
options: A Element R does not conduct electricity.
B Elements R and Y react together to form an ionic compound.
C Element Z exists as a diatomic molecule.
D Element Z reacts with element T.

questions.question: Lithium, sodium and potassium are elements in Group I of the Periodic Table. Which statement about sodium is correct? A Sodium is more dense than potassium., B Sodium reacts with water more vigorously than lithium., C Sodium has a lower melting point than potassium., D Solid sodium does not conduct electricity.
questions.text: Lithium, sodium and potassium are elements in Group I of the Periodic Table. Which statement about sodium is correct? A Sodium is more dense than potassium., B Sodium reacts with water more vigorously than lithium., C Sodium has a lower melting point than potassium., D Solid sodium does not conduct electricity.
options: A Sodium is more dense than potassium.
B Sodium reacts with water more vigorously than lithium.
C Sodium has a lower melting point than potassium.
D Solid sodium does not conduct electricity.

questions.question: Which property of transition elements is different from the properties of Group I metals? A They conduct electricity., B They are malleable., C They form coloured compounds., D They form basic oxides.
questions.text: Which property of transition elements is different from the properties of Group I metals? A They conduct electricity., B They are malleable., C They form coloured compounds., D They form basic oxides.

questions.question: The noble gases are in Group VIII of the Periodic Table. Which statement explains why noble gases are unreactive? A They all have eight electrons in their outer shells., B They all have full outer shells., C They are all gases., D They are all monoatomic. 9
questions.text: The noble gases are in Group VIII of the Periodic Table. Which statement explains why noble gases are unreactive? A They all have eight electrons in their outer shells., B They all have full outer shells., C They are all gases., D They are all monoatomic. 9
options: A They all have eight electrons in their outer shells.
B They all have full outer shells.

questions.question: Which statement is correct for all metals? A They conduct electricity when molten., B They gain electrons when they form ions., C They have a low density., D They have a low melting point.
questions.text: Which statement is correct for all metals? A They conduct electricity when molten., B They gain electrons when they form ions., C They have a low density., D They have a low melting point.
options: A They conduct electricity when molten.
B They gain electrons when they form ions.
C They have a low density.
D They have a low melting point.

questions.question: Chromium is a more reactive metal than iron but less reactive than zinc. Which statements are correct? 1   Chromium does not react with dilute hydrochloric acid. 2   Chromium oxide is reduced when it is heated with carbon. 3   Chromium reacts with zinc oxide to form zinc. 4   Chromium reacts with steam to form hydrogen gas. A 1 and 2 B  1 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4
questions.text: Chromium is a more reactive metal than iron but less reactive than zinc. Which statements are correct? 1   Chromium does not react with dilute hydrochloric acid. 2   Chromium oxide is reduced when it is heated with carbon. 3   Chromium reacts with zinc oxide to form zinc. 4   Chromium reacts with steam to form hydrogen gas. A 1 and 2 B  1 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4
options: A 1 and 2 B  1 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4

questions.question: Some properties of copper are listed. 1   It conducts electricity. 2   It conducts heat. 3   It is ductile. 4   It has a high melting point. Which properties of copper make it useful as a cooking pan? A 1 and 2 B  1 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4
questions.text: Some properties of copper are listed. 1   It conducts electricity. 2   It conducts heat. 3   It is ductile. 4   It has a high melting point. Which properties of copper make it useful as a cooking pan? A 1 and 2 B  1 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4
options: A 1 and 2 B  1 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4

questions.text: Sulfur burns to make sulfur dioxide.
